name: Leftover Turkey or Chicken Hash
recipeInstructions:
1. In a frying pan, heat 2-3 tablespoons of melted butter or turkey/chicken fat over medium heat.
2. Add thinly sliced onion and diced celery. Cook for 5-8 minutes, stirring often.
3. Add the remaining cooked turkey. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Prepare the sauce: In a separate bowl, brown the chicken fat (or use butter) with flour well before adding water. Stir in savory seasoning, salt, and pepper to taste. Gradually add water and mix until smooth and creamy. Stir in 1/4 cup cream for added richness.
5. Pour the sauce over the turkey mixture and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Serve hot biscuits alongside the hash and pickled beets.
Hot Biscuits: Sift together in a bowl, all-purpose flour, baking powder (use exactly as measured), and salt. In another bowl, whisk cream with 2 beaten eggs. Add to the dry ingredients just until moistened; the dough will be soft and lumpy. Stir only enough to combine. Drop spoonfuls onto a greased cookie sheet and bake at 400°F for about 16 minutes.
